document.name: microservice-dev-session
document.title: Microservice dev-session: provision a live dev preview, then hand off to an interactive coding agent
document.summary: Runs the five catalog-backed workflow-builder microservices (workflow-builder, workflow-orchestrator, function-router, mcp-gateway, and workflow-mcp-server) in an isolated preview-native vCluster development environment. It clones one immutable source revision into /sandbox/work, writes the multi-service sync helper, then hands the shared workspace and live preview to a persistent interactive coding-agent session for hot-reload development and full-system inspection.

service.title: Microservice to develop
service.description: workflow-builder | workflow-orchestrator | function-router | mcp-gateway | workflow-mcp-server (catalog-backed preview services).

mode.title: Dev-preview mode
mode.description: preview-native runs inside an isolated Tier-2 vCluster and adopts each selected service for live edits. host-throwaway remains an explicit compatibility mode for legacy host-cluster sessions.

keepPreview.title: Keep the preview alive after the run

artifacts.from: ${ { service: (.data.service // ""), url: (.data.url // ""), browseUrl: (.data.browseUrl // ""), syncUrl: (.data.syncUrl // ""), podIP: (.data.podIP // ""), ready: (.data.ready // false) } }
artifacts.title: Per-run dev preview

with.command:
${ "export REPOURL=" + ((.trigger.repoUrl // "PittampalliOrg/workflow-builder") | @sh) + "; export SOURCE_REVISION=" + ((.trigger.sourceRevision // "") | @sh) + "; export MODE=" + ((.trigger.mode // "preview-native") | @sh) + "; export PREVIEWS_B64=" + (((.provision_preview.services // [{service: (.provision_preview.service // .trigger.service // "workflow-orchestrator"), ok: (.provision_preview.ready // false), info: .provision_preview}]) | @json | @base64) | @sh) + ";" + "
set -eu
cd /sandbox/work
rm -rf repo .syncenv .syncenv.d .preview-services.json .preview-services-summary .sparse-paths .sparse-cones .sparse-cones.unsorted
test "$REPOURL" = PittampalliOrg/workflow-builder || { echo 'repoUrl must be PittampalliOrg/workflow-builder' >&2; exit 2; }
case "$MODE" in host-throwaway|preview-native) ;; *) echo 'unsupported dev-preview mode' >&2; exit 2 ;; esac
if [ "$MODE" = preview-native ]; then printf '%s' "$SOURCE_REVISION" | grep -Eq '^[0-9a-f]{40}$' || { echo 'preview-native requires a lowercase 40-hex sourceRevision' >&2; exit 2; }; fi
python3 - <<'PY_PREVIEW_METADATA'
import base64
import binascii
import json
import os
import posixpath
import re
import shlex
import sys
from pathlib import Path

MAX_ENCODED_BYTES = 1024 * 1024
MAX_DECODED_BYTES = 768 * 1024
MAX_SERVICES = 32
SAFE_SERVICE = re.compile(r"^[A-Za-z0-9][A-Za-z0-9._-]{0,127}$")
SAFE_PATH = re.compile(r"^[A-Za-z0-9._+-]+(?:/[A-Za-z0-9._+-]+)*$")


def invalid():
    raise ValueError("invalid preview metadata")


def safe_text(value, *, allow_empty=False, limit=8192):
    if not isinstance(value, str) or len(value) > limit:
        invalid()
    if not allow_empty and not value:
        invalid()
    if any(ord(char) < 32 or ord(char) == 127 for char in value):
        invalid()
    return value


def safe_path(value):
    value = safe_text(value, limit=512)
    if SAFE_PATH.fullmatch(value) is None:
        invalid()
    return value


def mappings(value):
    if not isinstance(value, list) or len(value) > 128:
        invalid()
    result = []
    for entry in value:
        if not isinstance(entry, dict):
            invalid()
        result.append((safe_path(entry.get("from")), safe_path(entry.get("to"))))
    return result


def repository_path(base, relative):
    resolved = posixpath.normpath(posixpath.join(base, relative))
    if (
        not resolved
        or resolved in (".", "..")
        or resolved.startswith("../")
        or posixpath.isabs(resolved)
    ):
        invalid()
    return resolved.removeprefix("./")


def write_private(path, content):
    flags = os.O_WRONLY | os.O_CREAT | os.O_TRUNC | getattr(os, "O_NOFOLLOW", 0)
    fd = os.open(path, flags, 0o600)
    with os.fdopen(fd, "w", encoding="utf-8", newline="\n") as handle:
        handle.write(content)
    os.chmod(path, 0o600)


try:
    encoded = os.environ.get("PREVIEWS_B64", "")
    if not encoded or len(encoded) > MAX_ENCODED_BYTES:
        invalid()
    decoded = base64.b64decode(encoded, validate=True)
    if not decoded or len(decoded) > MAX_DECODED_BYTES:
        invalid()
    previews = json.loads(decoded.decode("utf-8"))
    if not isinstance(previews, list) or not 1 <= len(previews) <= MAX_SERVICES:
        invalid()

    seen = set()
    services = []
    sparse_paths = {"scripts/dev-sync/sync.sh"}
    sync_files = []
    for entry in previews:
        if not isinstance(entry, dict) or entry.get("ok") is not True:
            invalid()
        service = entry.get("service")
        if not isinstance(service, str) or SAFE_SERVICE.fullmatch(service) is None:
            invalid()
        if service in seen:
            invalid()
        seen.add(service)

        info = entry.get("info")
        if not isinstance(info, dict) or info.get("ready") is not True:
            invalid()
        repo_subdir = safe_path(info.get("repoSubdir", "."))
        sync_paths = info.get("syncPaths", ["src"])
        if not isinstance(sync_paths, list) or not 1 <= len(sync_paths) <= 128:
            invalid()
        sync_paths = [safe_path(path) for path in sync_paths]
        sync_url = safe_text(info.get("syncUrl"), limit=2048)
        if re.fullmatch(r"https?://[^\s]+", sync_url) is None:
            invalid()
        sync_token = safe_text(
            info.get("syncCapability", ""),
            allow_empty=True,
            limit=16384,
        )
        extra_sync = mappings(info.get("extraSync", []))
        capture_only = mappings(info.get("captureOnly", []))
        combined_mappings = extra_sync + capture_only

        for path in sync_paths:
            sparse_paths.add(repository_path(repo_subdir, path))
        for source, _target in combined_mappings:
            sparse_paths.add(repository_path(repo_subdir, source))

        env_values = {
            "SERVICE": service,
            "SUBDIR": repo_subdir,
            "PATHS": " ".join(sync_paths),
            "SYNCURL": sync_url,
            "SYNC_TOKEN": sync_token,
            "EXTRASYNC": " ".join(
                f"{source}:{target}" for source, target in combined_mappings
            ),
        }
        sync_files.append(
            (
                service,
                "".join(
                    f"{key}={shlex.quote(value)}\n"
                    for key, value in env_values.items()
                ),
            )
        )
        services.append(service)

    sync_dir = Path(".syncenv.d")
    sync_dir.mkdir(mode=0o700)
    os.chmod(sync_dir, 0o700)
    write_private(
        ".preview-services.json",
        json.dumps(previews, ensure_ascii=True, separators=(",", ":")) + "\n",
    )
    write_private(".sparse-paths", "".join(f"{path}\n" for path in sorted(sparse_paths)))
    for service, content in sync_files:
        write_private(sync_dir / service, content)
    write_private(".preview-services-summary", ",".join(services) + "\n")
except (binascii.Error, OSError, TypeError, UnicodeError, ValueError):
    print("failed to materialize trusted preview service metadata", file=sys.stderr)
    raise SystemExit(4)
PY_PREVIEW_METADATA
test -s .sparse-paths || { echo 'preview source metadata produced no checkout paths' >&2; exit 4; }
LOCAL_ROOT=$(mktemp -d /tmp/wfb-preview-checkout.XXXXXX)
trap 'rm -rf "$LOCAL_ROOT"' EXIT HUP INT TERM
CLONE_URL="https://github.com/$REPOURL.git"
export GIT_TERMINAL_PROMPT=0
if [ -n "${GITHUB_TOKEN:-}" ]; then
  GIT_ASKPASS="$LOCAL_ROOT/git-askpass"
  printf '%s\n' '#!/bin/sh' 'case "$1" in' '  *Username*) printf "%s\n" x-access-token ;;' '  *Password*) printf "%s\n" "$GITHUB_TOKEN" ;;' '  *) exit 1 ;;' 'esac' > "$GIT_ASKPASS"
  chmod 700 "$GIT_ASKPASS"
  export GIT_ASKPASS
fi
LOCAL_REPO="$LOCAL_ROOT/repo"
git clone --filter=blob:none --no-checkout --depth 1 --single-branch "$CLONE_URL" "$LOCAL_REPO"
if [ -n "${GITHUB_TOKEN:-}" ]; then
  git -C "$LOCAL_REPO" config credential.username x-access-token
  git -C "$LOCAL_REPO" config credential.helper '!f() { test -n "$GITHUB_TOKEN" || exit 1; printf "%s\n" "password=$GITHUB_TOKEN"; }; f'
fi
if [ -n "$SOURCE_REVISION" ]; then
  printf '%s' "$SOURCE_REVISION" | grep -Eq '^[0-9a-f]{40}$' || { echo 'sourceRevision must be a lowercase 40-hex Git SHA' >&2; exit 2; }
  git -C "$LOCAL_REPO" fetch --no-tags --depth 1 origin "$SOURCE_REVISION"
  TARGET_REVISION="$SOURCE_REVISION"
else
  TARGET_REVISION=$(git -C "$LOCAL_REPO" rev-parse HEAD)
fi
: > .sparse-cones.unsorted
while IFS= read -r path; do
  TREE_ENTRY=$(git -C "$LOCAL_REPO" ls-tree "$TARGET_REVISION" -- "$path") || { echo 'failed to inspect preview source path at target revision' >&2; exit 4; }
  SOURCE_TYPE=$(printf '%s\n' "$TREE_ENTRY" | awk 'NR == 1 { print $2 }')
  case "$SOURCE_TYPE" in
    tree) printf '%s\n' "$path" >> .sparse-cones.unsorted ;;
    blob)
      case "$path" in
        */*) printf '%s\n' "${path%/*}" >> .sparse-cones.unsorted ;;
      esac
      ;;
    "") printf '%s\n' "$path" >> .sparse-cones.unsorted ;;
    *) echo 'preview source metadata resolved to an unsupported Git object' >&2; exit 4 ;;
  esac
done < .sparse-paths
sort -u .sparse-cones.unsorted > .sparse-cones
git -C "$LOCAL_REPO" sparse-checkout init --cone
git -C "$LOCAL_REPO" sparse-checkout set --stdin < .sparse-cones
git -C "$LOCAL_REPO" checkout --detach "$TARGET_REVISION"
ACTUAL_REVISION=$(git -C "$LOCAL_REPO" rev-parse HEAD)
if [ -n "$SOURCE_REVISION" ]; then test "$ACTUAL_REVISION" = "$SOURCE_REVISION" || { echo 'checked-out revision does not match sourceRevision' >&2; exit 3; }; fi
git -C "$LOCAL_REPO" remote set-url origin "$CLONE_URL"
tar -C "$LOCAL_REPO" -cf "$LOCAL_ROOT/repo.tar" .
mkdir repo
tar -C repo -xf "$LOCAL_ROOT/repo.tar"
test "$(git -C repo rev-parse HEAD)" = "$ACTUAL_REVISION" || { echo 'materialized revision does not match local checkout' >&2; exit 3; }
cp repo/scripts/dev-sync/sync.sh /sandbox/work/sync.sh
chmod +x /sandbox/work/sync.sh
IFS= read -r SERVICES_SUMMARY < .preview-services-summary
echo CLONED "$ACTUAL_REVISION"
echo SERVICES "$SERVICES_SUMMARY"
" }

with.instructions:
${ "You are the interactive developer for these microservices: **" + ((.trigger.services // (if .trigger.service then [.trigger.service] else ["workflow-builder", "workflow-orchestrator", "function-router", "mcp-gateway", "workflow-mcp-server"] end)) | join(", ")) + "**.

- One monorepo checkout is at /sandbox/work/repo. The per-service source mappings are in /sandbox/work/.syncenv.d/.
- The full isolated system is already running. Edit any selected service in that checkout, then run /sandbox/work/sync.sh once; it pushes one logical generation to every selected service and hot-reloads UI and backend processes.
- sync.sh detects dependency-manifest changes and runs the cataloged dependency action. Cataloged in-pod tests are available through each service's /__run endpoint. After syncing a workflow-builder drizzle/ change, run its allowlisted migrate action before testing the live schema.
- Run the relevant fast tests after edits, then inspect the functioning system at " + (.trigger.previewOrigin // "the provisioned browse URL") + ". Exercise cross-service behavior, not only a standalone health route.
- Do not commit or push from the session. Atomic capture and PR promotion are separate workflow actions.

Reply 'ready' and wait for the requested feature. Then implement it across the selected services, run sync.sh, run the allowlisted tests, and verify the live system." }
